<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom Ocsp Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the trust store</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the trust store</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the trust store</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If set to <code>true</code>, no certificate validations are performed, which renders connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the key store</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key to use. If not defined, the first key in the file is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the private key</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the key store</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the key store</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Verify only the last element of the certificate chain</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Try CRL (instead of OCSP) first</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Ocsp Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The URL of the OCSP responder</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Header

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Query Param

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Inbound WebSocket Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP version of the request</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Local Address</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Local host address from the server</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Remote Address</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Remote host address from the sender</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Certificate</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Client certificate (if two-way TLS is enabled)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket ID</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>INBOUND</p></li><li><p>OUTBOUND</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket type</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Groups</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The groups on which this socket is subscribed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Full path where the request was received</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Full URI of the request</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheme</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>WS</p></li><li><p>WSS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP scheme of the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Params</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Query parameters map built from the parsed string</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Headers map built from the parsed string</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Uri Params</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URI parameters extracted from the request path</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Method</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP method of the request</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Query string of the request</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of memory to allocate to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can be fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering a process-failed-message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SHA-256</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Identifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines which strategy is used to identify the messages</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is stored</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property can be set only if useSecureHash is false.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### WebSocket Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket ID</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>INBOUND</p></li><li><p>OUTBOUND</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket type</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Groups</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The groups to which this socket is subscribed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Full path where the request was received</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Full URI of the request</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheme</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>WS</p></li><li><p>WSS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP scheme of the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Params</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Query parameters map built from the parsed string</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Headers map built from the parsed string</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Uri Params</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URI parameters extracted from the request path</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Method</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP method of the request</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Query string of the request</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Closed Connection Event

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Code</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NORMAL_CLOSURE</p></li><li><p>ENDPOINT_GOING_DOWN</p></li><li><p>PROTOCOL_ERROR</p></li><li><p>INVALID_DATA</p></li><li><p>MESSAGE_TOO_LARGE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The close code</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reason</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The phrase with the reason the socket was closed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Broadcast Failure

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Error Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Exception</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>INBOUND</p></li><li><p>OUTBOUND</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Proxy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host where the proxy requests is sent</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port where the proxy requests is sent</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The username to authenticate against the proxy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password to authenticate against the proxy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non Proxy Hosts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A list of comma-separated hosts against which the proxy should not be used</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### NTLM Proxy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Ntlm Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The domain to authenticate against the proxy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host where the proxy requests is sent</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port where the proxy requests is sent</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The username to authenticate against the proxy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password to authenticate against the proxy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non Proxy Hosts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A list of comma-separated hosts against which the proxy should not be used</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [WebSocket Connector Overview](./)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# WebSockets Server Components

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Inbound Listener

The Inbound Listener component represents a WebSocket endpoint that clients can connect to. By
browsing to this endpoint, external clients establish a WebSocket connection, and trigger the flow
each time they send a message.

Clients can also indicate the default groups that this endpoint automatically subscribes to in the
defaultGroups parameter. If any broadcast operations are performed to these groups, this endpoint
receives and processes them. This subscription is done inside the app domain, which means that
adding the default groups parameter dows not send information about this subscription to the
outbound WebSocket counterparts. This is because the groups feature is not part of WS protocol. When
you subscribe an inbound WebSocket to a group, if one WebSocket within your app domain wants to send
a message to all other WebSockets, your app domain also has to be subscribed to the specified
groups. In short, group subscription only affects WebSockets residing in the same app domain, and
WebSockets from external applications are not subscribed by a Mule app to any other groups.

```xml
<flow name="onIncomingMessageFlow">
    <websocket:inbound-listener
        path="/chat"
        defaultGroups="#[['AVENGERS', 'REVENGERS']]"
        config-ref="ws" />
    <logger
        level="INFO"
        message="#['Received message ' ++ payload ++ ' from socket: ' ++ attributes.socketId]" />

    <websocket:send
        doc:name="SendResponse"
        socketId="#[attributes.socketId]"
        config-ref="ws" >
        <websocket:content>#['I'm fine, thank you!']</websocket:content>
    </websocket:send>
</flow>

<flow name="sendFriendlyMessageFlow">
    <websocket:open-outbound-socket path="/chat" config-ref="ws" />

    <websocket:send
        doc:name="SendMessage"
        socketId="#[attributes.socketId]"
        config-ref="ws" >
        <websocket:content>#['Hi! How are you?']</websocket:content>
    </websocket:send>
</flow>
```

## Send a Response

Unlike the `<http:listener>` element that automatically sends a response when a flow ends, this
component never automatically sends a response. This is because the WebSocket protocol is full
duplex and therefore provides no guarantee that you need to or want to send a response. Depending on
your use case, you might want to process the messages without sending a response, or only send
responses on certain cases.

If you want to send a response, use the `<websocket:send>` operation.

### Output

Each time a new message arrives, the flow triggers with a message, and the payload contains the
messageâs content. The messageâs MIME type is obtained from the `Content-Type` header of the HTTP
request. If the MIME type hasnât been set, the MIME type defaults to one of the following depending
on the protocolâs DataFrame type:

- If binary, defaults MIME to `application/octect-stream`
- If text, defaults MIME to `text/plain`

The attributes contain a WebSockets attributes object.

## On Inbound Connection

This is a message source that triggers each time an INBOUND WebSockets connection is established.
You can only have one of these sources for a given path and it requires that a matching inbound
listener exist for the path. This is useful to perform post-connect tasks such as logging or
assigning the socket with a group.

This flow triggers synchronously. By the time this flow is invoked, the socket has already been
established and it could potentially be broadcasting or receiving messages. Those messages are
queued and not delivered to any flow until the event triggered by the source completes. If it fails,
all queued messages are discarded and the connection is dropped.

This message source has two main use cases:

- Keep track of the generated socket IDs for later use
- Have a validation point in which WebSockets can be rejected before it becomes usable to the
  application

For example, the following flow validates attributes from the originating request and decides to
close the connection if the validation doesnât pass.

```xml
<flow name="onNewInboundConnection">
    <websocket:on-inbound-connection
        doc:name="On Inbound Connection"
        config-ref="WebSockets_Config"
        path="/chat"/>
    <choice doc:name="Choice" >
        <when expression="#[contains(attributes.headers.alias, 'admin')]">
            <websocket:close socketId="#[attributes.socketId]"
                reason="Cannot impersonate the admin"
                config-ref="WebSockets_Config"/>
        </when>
        <otherwise >
            <logger
                level="INFO"
                doc:name="Logger"
                message="Successfully connected to WebSocket #[attributes.socketId]" />
        </otherwise>
    </choice>
</flow>

<flow name="chat">
    <websocket:inbound-listener
        doc:name="Inbound listener"
        path="/chat"
        config-ref="WebSockets_Config"/>
    <logger
        level="INFO"
        doc:name="Logger"
        message="#['Received message' ++ payload]"/>
</flow>
```

## See Also

- [WebSockets Connector Overview](./)
- [Server Components](#)
- [Messaging Operations](websockets-connector-messaging-operations)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# WebSockets Studio Configuration

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To configure the connector in Anypoint Studio:

1.  Add the connector to a Studio project.
2.  Configure the connector.
3.  Configure an input source for the connector.

## Add the Connector to a Studio Project

Anypoint Studio provides two ways to add the connector to your Studio project: from the Exchange
button in the Studio taskbar or from the Mule Palette view.

### Add the Connector Using Exchange

1.  In Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  Click the Exchange icon **(X)** in the upper-left of the Studio task bar.
3.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
4.  In Exchange, search for "websockets".
5.  Select the connector and click **Add to project**.
6.  Follow the prompts to install the connector.

### Add the Connector in Studio

1.  In Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the Mule Palette view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type "websockets" in the search field.
4.  Click this connectorâs name in **Available modules**.
5.  Click **Add**.
6.  Click **Finish**.

## Configure the Connector

1.  Go to the **Global Elements** tab, and click **Create**.
2.  From **Connector Configuration**, select the **WebSockets Config**.
3.  If you want your connector to listen for incoming requests check the **Configure Inbound
    WebSocket** section.  
    If you want to perform requests to external services, check the **Configure Outbound WebSocket**
    section.  
    You can check both if your application will be doing both inbound and outbound connections.

### Configure Inbound WebSockets

1.  Go to the **Global Elements** tab and click **Create**.
2.  From **Connector Configuration**, select the **HTTP Listener Config**.
3.  In your WebSockets Configuration, check the **Server settings** box.
4.  Specify the listener base path(optional) and reference the HTTP Listener Config you created
    before.

### Configure Outbound WebSockets

1.  In your WebSockets Configuration, check the **Client settings** box.
2.  Specify the following optional properties:
    - Host
    - Port
    - Path

3.  In the **Default Headers** and **Query Parameters** sections you can introduce your default
    values for headers and query parameters by selecting an option from the drop-down list.
    1.  Select **Expression** to enter a `dwl` expression.
    2.  Click **Edit Inline** to enter key/value pairs by clicking the '+' sign.

## Configure an Input Source

Configure an input source for the connector.

You can put the following operations for the connector in the **Source** area of the Studio canvas:

- Inbound Listener
- On Outbound Socket Message
- On Inbound Connection (requires an Inbound Listener)
- On Socket Closed

## Next

Now that you have completed configuring Studio, see the
[Connector Configuration](websockets-connector-config-topics) for more configuration ideas.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [WebSockets Connector Overview](./)
- [XML and Maven Configuration](websockets-connector-xml-maven)
---

# Troubleshooting WebSockets Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To troubleshoot Anypoint Connector for WebSockets (WebSockets Connector), become familiar with the
information about enabling verbose logging and interpreting commonly thrown messages.

## Enable Verbose Logging

To get a better understanding of why an applicationâs interaction with WebSockets Connector is
failing, temporarily enable verbose logging for the connector.

Remember to always disable the enhanced verbosity after troubleshooting because it affects your Mule
application performance.

To enable verbose logging in the configuration file:

1.  Access Anypoint Studio and navigate to the **Package Explorer** view.
2.  Open your applicationâs project name.
3.  Open the `src/main/resources` path folder.
4.  Open the `log4j2.xml` file inside the folder.
5.  Add an `<AsyncLogger>` tag inside the `<Loggers>` tag:

    ```xml
    			<Loggers>
    				...
    				<AsyncLogger name="com.mulesoft.connectors.ws" level="DEBUG"/>
    				...
    			</Loggers>
    ```

6.  Save your application changes.
7.  Click the project name in **Package Explorer** and then click **Run** > **Run As** > **Mule
    Application**.

## Understand Common Throws

Here is a list of common throw messages and how to interpret them:

- WEBSOCKET:CONNECTIVITY

  The connection is no longer valid. Apply a reconnection policy if defined in the application
  setup.

- WEBSOCKET:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

  The maximum number of retries for the operation is reached.

- WEBSOCKET:NO_SUCH_SOCKET

  The WebSocket cannot be referenced because the WebSocket ID does not exist.

- WEBSOCKET:BASIC_AUTHENTICATION

  The HTTP \*Request\* operation lacks basic authentication to send requests to the service, or the
  provided credentials are incorrect.

- WEBSOCKET:FORBIDDEN

  The server refuses to give the requested resource because the client does not have access rights
  to the content.

- WEBSOCKET:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

  The server encountered a problem that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

- WEBSOCKET:INVALID_SOCKET_ID

  The WebSocket ID is null or blank.

- WEBSOCKET:NON_UNIQUE_SOCKET_ID

  An error occurred while registering the WebSocket because the ID already exists.

- WEBSOCKET:NOT_FOUND

  The server could not find the requested resource.

- WEBSOCKET:REMOTELY_CLOSED

  The remote host closed the connection, which could be related to an SSL/TLS handshake issue. Check
  protocols, cipher suites, and certificate setup. Use -Djavax.net.debug=ssl for further debugging.

- WEBSOCKET:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE

  The server is unable to manage the request because it is down for maintenance or overloaded.

- WEBSOCKET:UNAUTHORIZED

  The authentication failed or has not yet been provided to get the requested response.

## See Also

- [WebSockets Connector Reference](websockets-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
---

# WebSockets Connector XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio.

If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML, either from the Anypoint Studio XML
editor or from a text editor, you can get access to the connector in your app by adding reference
statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.

When you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location. It also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/websocket
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/websocket/current/mule-websocket.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the `<mule>` XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:websocket="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/websocket"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/websocket
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/websocket/current/mule-websocket.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
If you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to
this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-websockets-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `websockets`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## Next Step

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the
[Examples](websockets-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
